rulings.Case Title: STANLEY CILETTI ET AL VS. PARK LANE ASSOCIATES, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of 05/17/2022 Order, And For An Order Staying Enforcement Of The Arbitration Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2022 line 1 DEFENDANT 1100 SACRAMENTO STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion For Clarification Of 05/17/2022 Order, And For An Order Staying Enforcement Of The Arbitration Order DENIED. The motion is premature. Cross-defendant, cross-complainant and defendant, 1100 Sacramento HOA seeks clarification whether this Court intended to order the cross-complaints between the co-defendants to be arbitrated. No order on file as to the motion to compel arbitration as on August 15, 2022. The Court's May 17, 2022 ruling ordered Plaintiff's claims against defendants into arbitration staying the rest of the litigation and not any cross-complaints involved in this litigation. This May 17, 2022 ruling was never reduced to a written order. Accordingly, the Court of Appeal remanded the writ brought by cross-complaint and cross-defendant 1100 Sacramento HOA because there was no order pursuant to the Court's May 17, 2022 ruling. On May 17, 2022 the Court adopted its tentative ruling ("Plaintiff's claims against both defendants shall proceed in arbitration") and ordered the parties to comply with CRC 3.1312 regarding the final order. The Court's records do not indicate any submissions compliant with CRC 3.1312 (or any submissions of a proposed order at all, compliant or otherwise, from moving or opposing parties). Therefore, there is still no order on file regarding Motion to Compel, rendering this request for clarification of order premature. The original Notice of Motion to Compel Arbitration filed on February 28, 2022 sought to compel arbitration for the "entirety of all claims between all parties in this dispute." All claims between the parties as of February 28, 2022 (the date the motion to compel was filed) included Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint filed on October 22, 20221; Park Lane LLC's Cross-Complaint filed on October 23, 2021; and 1100 Sacramento HOA's Cross-Complaint filed on February 14, 2022. TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUES ON NEXT MOTION ENTRY=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY CILETTI ET AL VS. PARK LANE ASSOCIATES, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2022 line 1 Continued tentative ruling for DEFENDANT 1100 SACRAMENTO STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion For Clarification Of 05/17/2022 Order, And For An Order Staying Enforcement Of The Arbitration Order The Court's tentative ruling issued on May 16, 2022, and adopted after argument on May 17, 2022, states in the part relevant to arbitration that only "[p]laintiff's claims against both defendants shall proceed in arbitration." The moving party in the present motion for clarification is the HOA. 1100 Sacramento HOA is a defendant, a cross-defendant and a cross-complainant in this action. This party is not and never has been a Plaintiff in this action. The clarification sought in this motion only pertains to the cross-complaints, and not to any claims by the Plaintiff against the HOA as a defendant. As specified in the May 16, 2022 tentative ruling, only Plaintiff's claims against defendants are to proceed in arbitration. Cross-complaints are specifically excluded from the ruling (compare the Notice of Motion and the Ruling). The rest of the litigation (i.e. cross-complaints) is stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration. Park Lane is ordered to prepare and circulate a proposed order on the Motion to Compel Arbitration per CRC 3.1312(b) no later than August 16, 2022 and provide the order to the Court with objections/competing orders, if any, no later than August 26, 2022. 1100 Sacramento may renew its motion for clarification and stay if necessary and proper after a final order is entered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GEARY-NINTH DE, LLC VS. KEVIN LIEW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT KEVIN LIEW'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND DENIED. Defendant was properly served by a registered process server. Defendant was at the premises after July 6, 2022 and before July 20, 2022 and did not attend to the Summons and Complaint posted on his door and remaining on his door as of July 7, 2022. Defendant fails to meet the burden both under CCP 473.5 and CCP 473(b). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
